input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-11T01:01:53.508Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your MAUI page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` watches the OS theme and swaps values automatically — no event handling needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));
//                                         ↑ light value   ↑ dark value

BackgroundColor = Application.Current.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark
    ? Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
    : Colors.White;
// But prefer SetAppTheme — it reacts to live theme changes:
this.SetAppTheme(BackgroundColorProperty, Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

## Key points

| Concern | Solution |
|---|---|
| **XAML** | `{AppThemeBinding Light=..., Dark=...}` |
| **C#** | `element.SetAppTheme(property, lightVal, darkVal)` |
| **Resources** | Use `AppThemeBinding` inside `ResourceDictionary` styles to theme globally |
| **Live switching** | Both approaches react automatically when the user toggles the OS theme |

For app-wide consistency, define themed styles in `App.xaml`:

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <Style TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
    </Style>
</Application.Resources>
```

This keeps individual pages clean and ensures uniform theming.
